reasoning: Base rate of a stock going up over 1 to 4 weeks is around 54-55%. CTAS has high operating margins, strong growth (recently reported Q2 revenue +9.3%), and a steady long-term upward trajectory. The historical time series of its prices shows an upward slope (0.14/day) with a statistical estimate of P=0.54-0.60 to be higher in late Feb 2026. The stock recently pulled back from ~$200 down to $193.4 (the benchmark price). We know the ex-dividend date passed on February 13, 2026 for a $0.45 dividend, which explains part of the drop on Feb 13. There will be no ex-dividend cut before March 17. The Q3 earnings are due in March (often March 18 or later, as they report "in March" and April 01 is estimated), which might create volatility around March 17 but isn’t definitively negative. Since $193.4 is already somewhat reduced due to the recent $0.45 ex-dividend action and a multi-day minor pullback, I expect a modest drift upwards. P(>193.4) at +1 week is set to 0.56, and at +1 month is set to 0.59.
